items.title: Dit bedrag houdt Go Ahead Eagles over aan de EL-campagne
items.summary: Nu de Europa League-campagne van Go Ahead Eagles erop zit, kunnen we het financiële plaatje vaststellen. Wat heeft de Europese deelname de Deventer equipe opgeleverd?

items.title: Feyenoord op Rapport: missende Larin ver onder de maat
items.summary: Real Betis - Feyenoord is geëindigd in een overwinning voor de Spanjaarden en uitschakeling in de Europa League van de Rotterdammers, maar het had heel anders kunnen lopen. Met name Cyle Larin kreeg kansen namens Feyenoord. Hij benutte ze niet en dat is te zien in de rapportcijfers.

items.title: Go Ahead op Rapport: iedereen een voldoende bij Europees afscheid
items.summary: Go Ahead Eagles kan Europa met opgeheven hoofd verlaten. Donderdagavond hielden de Deventenaren in eigen huis stand tegen SC Braga ( 0-0 ). Het leverde alle spelers van Kowet een voldoende op.

items.title: Lyon wint competitiefase Europa League, AS Roma ontloopt tussenronde
items.summary: Olympique Lyon is bovenaan geëindigd in de competitiefase van de Europa League. De Fransen dreigden punten te verspelen tegen PAOK Saloniki, maar de ploeg van trainer Paulo Fonseca won alsnog. Aston Villa werd tweede, FC Midtjylland derde.

items.title: Valente aangeslagen na blessure Van Persie: 'Verschrikkelijk om te zien'
items.summary: Luciano Valente stond na de nederlaag tegen Real Betis ( 2-1 ) verbeten voor de camera, wetende dat een overwinning voldoende was geweest voor het bereiken van de tussenronde. Daarnaast was hij aangeslagen na de blessure van Shaqueel van Persie.

items.title: O’Neill delight as Celtic ease to Europa League playoff with win over Utrecht
items.summary: Celtic 4-2 Utrecht This was a little more fraught than it needed to be and looked as if it would be for Celtic. Still, Martin O’Neill can focus on the most important fact in that he has guided the Scottish champions into the Europa League’s playoff phase. Job done, once again, for the effervescent O’Neill. Celtic were fully deserving of their win in what became an entertaining clash. Their wobble in the second half proved to be only that. O’Neill, thought to be a managerial yesterday’s man not so long ago, continues to do his bit for 70-somethings everywhere. The Irishman will know Celtic must improve in order to make meaningful, further progress in this competition but such detail can wait. Even continuation in the Europa League had looked a long shot in the latter part of 2025. Utrecht will be delighted to see the back of a tournament which yielded just a single point from eight grisly fixtures. Continue reading...

items.title: Nottingham Forest seal playoff spot after Igor Jesus fells Ferencvaros
items.summary: Nottingham Forest cruised into the Europa League playoffs with a one-sided victory against Ferencvaros, Igor Jesus maintaining his impressive goalscoring form to cap a fine few days for Sean Dyche. The last time Forest won three successive home games in a European competition, in 1979, it culminated in them lifting their first European Cup under Brian Clough. While there is a long way to go in this competition, these are the kind of nights that foster hope of at least reaching the showpiece in Istanbul in May. James McAtee completed the scoring from the penalty spot to condemn Robbie Keane’s side to a 4-0 defeat. Continue reading...

items.title: Celtic beat Utrecht to make Europa League playoffs
items.summary: Celtic beat Utrecht 4-2 at Parkhead to earn a place in next month's Europa League playoffs.

items.title: USWNT legend Dunn announces retirement at 33

items.summary: Buffalo Bills quarterback Josh Allen has revealed that he recently had surgery for a broken bone in his right foot, but expects to be fully healed for the start of spring practices.

items.title: Crystal Palace stall on £50m Strand Larsen deal but close on Guessand loan
items.summary: Palace will not let Mateta go without replacement James Ward-Prowse seals loan move to Burnley Jørgen Strand Larsen’s £50m move from Wolves to south London is in serious doubt after Crystal Palace stalled on a deal on Thursday afternoon. Palace had verbally agreed in principle to sign the Norway striker for £45m, plus £5m in add-ons. Wolves were awaiting a formal written offer but it never arrived despite positive talks. Continue reading...

items.title: Palace move for £50m Strand Larsen in doubt
items.summary: Crystal Palace's £50m move for Wolves striker Jorgen Strand Larsen is in the balance after the Eagles suggest they could walk away from the deal.

items.title: How English clubs got smart to dominate this season's Champions League
items.summary: Premier League clubs always had the money, but now they also have the know-how they have dominated this season's Champions League.
